creator: lev@FreeBSD.org
text:
I'm choosing between "TI" (RedHat, really) sources and official stock upstream sources right now to update port at weekend :)

Peter Bigot (author of old, obsolete port) uses stock one.

And sources from RedHat package looks terrible — no version, strange sources version, hacked build system, etc.

Upstream sources doesn't have some patches (nobody knows how critical ones!), but could be built separately (binutils / gcc / header files) and most of ports infrastructure (for cross-binutils, for example) could be reused.

Do you use MSP430 toolcahin on FreeBSD? I've moved to ARM32 microcontrolers now. Do you have preferences — preferences or TI/RedHat?
